metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4055
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 3: Representative recordings of single-cell [Ca2+]i responses to KCl depolarization, in the presence of (a-c) NPY1R agonist [Leu31, Pro34]NPY.

Figure 4: Pre-synaptic modulation of [Ca2+]i by NPY receptors was tested on rat hippocampal synaptosomes. Fura-2 loaded synaptosomes were depolarized with KCl, resulting in a subsequent increase in [Ca2+]i. NPY1R agonist [Leu31,Pro34]NPY inhibited the increase in [Ca2+]i upon KCl stimulation.

Figure 6a: The total glutamate release was found to be significantly decreased upon addition of NPY1R agonist. And the addition of the selective Y1 receptor antagonist, BIBP3226, completely prevented the inhibitory effect induced by [Leu31, Pro34]NPY further solidifying the role of NPY1R in modulating glutamate release in hippocampal neurons.

Figure 7: The role of NPY1R in modulating glutamate release was specifically seen in CA3 and DAG, but not in the CA1 region of the hippocampus.

11/01/2021 Dnyanada: The paper demonstrates the involvement of NPY1R via binding of its ligand NPY in presynaptic inhibition of endogenous glutamate release. SynGO ID: 4035 (PMID: 29775703) shows that the NPY1R is a receptor specific for the neuropeptide NPY.. Experimental description: Hippocampal neurones were dissociated from hippocampi of E18-E19 Wistar rat embryos
